element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary control avatar; player learns mech piloting, fuel/ammo management, and basic movement
Player learns economy management; eight unit types with speed/anti-air/ground-attack/cost tradeoffs
Player coordinates mech transport of purchased units across battlefield

element_introduction_order.element: Mission assignment orders (patrol, garrison, capture, fixed position)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns tactical micromanagement; reassignment carries cost penalty
Nine permanent indestructible outposts scattered across map; key strategic resource for revenue and remote bases
Both mech and ground units have limited fuel/ammo; prolonged engagement requires careful supply management
Fragile supply vehicles with limited service radius; must manage repair and refuel for sustained operations
AI difficulty scales; armor and damage increase with advancing difficulty levels
Direct human competition; no screen sharing in multiplayer, intensifies real-time decision pressure

typical_relief_triggers: Returning mech to base for repair/refuel
typical_tension_triggers: Mech low on fuel or ammunition
Ground units under enemy fire without orders
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Real-time simultaneous action with constant micromanagement demands; tension perpetually present due to finite resources and need to think on move; relief comes through successful tactical repositioning rather than pauses

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Outpost locations scattered across battlefield; player chooses which to prioritize or deny enemy through tactical positioning

difficulty_curve.early_game: Learning curve steep but manageable; player introduced to mech control and basic unit purchasing in safe opening phase
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Complexity escalates through simultaneous airlift-deploy-order coordination; multiple outpost threats require strategic prioritization
difficulty_curve.late_game: AI armor and damage scaling creates sustained pressure; resource attrition warfare becomes dominant; multiplayer increasingly unforgiving
notable_spikes: First enemy unit engagement requiring coordinated mech + ground defense
Transition from single outpost control to multi-outpost territory management
AI difficulty level increases, intensifying armor/damage advantage
Supply logistics breaking down under multiple simultaneous engagements

notable_level_patterns.description: Core strategic pattern: player captures and holds nine scattered permanent outposts to generate revenue and deny enemy resources; outpost control directly gates economic capacity

All command functions (unit purchase, airlift, repair, refuel, order reassignment) funnel through player-piloted mech; creates forced micromanagement and vulnerability window during transport operations
Finite fuel and ammunition across all units creates perpetual scarcity tension; fragile supply vehicles limit sustained operations; encourages cautious positioning and strategic retreat
No pause in single-player; both players in multiplayer act simultaneously without decision pauses; forces constant tactical adaptation and reactive positioning
